title: Flag Memory
summary: A multiplayer game where everyone draws a flag from memory, gets an accuracy score and compares the result with the lobby.
intro: Flag Memory is the project I am closest to finishing. Players join or create a lobby, draw the shown flag from memory and get scored by comparing their drawing against the real flag.

cover.alt: Flag Memory score screen comparing a player's Barbados drawing to the original flag

details: I made the flag analysis for around 130 flags. It compares the player's drawing to the real flag and gives an accuracy score based on the pixels.
The canvas includes a paint brush, paint bucket, eraser, lines, rectangles, circles and stars.
The game has custom lobbies, live rankings, a final podium, player records and badges.
I made the scripting, UI, effects and game systems myself.

media.alt: Flag Memory player record and badge cabinet screen
media.title: Player records and badges

title: Sky Garden
summary: A farming game set on floating islands with crops, fishing, seedpacks, shops and island progression.
intro: Sky Garden is still early in development. The main idea is a relaxed farming game where the island slowly grows and more activities open up as the player progresses.

cover.alt: Sky Garden floating island with crops, trees and the player's avatar

details: Players plow the ground, plant seeds, water crops and wait for each crop to grow one part at a time.
The island expands through progression and later opens up more activities.
Fishing has its own timing and reward system.
Seedpacks and shops are used to find and buy different seeds.

title: Cube Wars
summary: A scrapped PvP game where cubes eat orbs, pick cards and use the abilities from those cards to fight other players.
intro: Cube Wars was a project I stopped working on. You played as a cube, collected orbs to get stronger and picked cards that changed what you could do in fights.

cover.alt: Cube Wars gameplay showing a cube levelling up

details: Players grew by collecting orbs around the map.
Cards gave the player new abilities and upgrades.
The cards could then be used to fight other players.
The project was scrapped, but I kept it here because I still like parts of the combat and progression.

title: Dialogue System
summary: A reusable dialogue system with controlled text pacing, response choices and branching conversations.
intro: I built this so dialogue could be reused for different NPCs without rebuilding the whole interface each time.

cover.alt: Dialogue interface shown in a Roblox game

details: Text can appear at a controlled pace instead of showing all at once.
Replies can branch into different parts of the conversation.
The layout can be restyled for different games and NPCs.
It works with shop and interaction flows as well as normal conversations.

title: Inventory System
summary: A custom inventory and hotbar with categories, search, item data and selected states.
intro: This replaces Roblox's default backpack with an inventory that fits the game and can handle more item information.

cover.alt: Custom Roblox inventory with categories, search and a hotbar

details: Items are split into categories and can be searched.
The inventory keeps track of item data, quantities and selected states.
The hotbar stays connected to the full inventory.
The interface was built around the game's own item types instead of Roblox's default backpack.

title: Wandering Trader
summary: A trader who flies onto the island in a hot-air balloon, wanders around and sells rare seeds for less.
intro: The wandering trader is a temporary visitor in Sky Garden. He arrives in a hot-air balloon, walks around the player's island and offers rare seeds for 30% cheaper than normal.

cover.alt: Wandering Trader arriving in a hot-air balloon

details: The trader arrives on the island using a hot-air balloon.
He wanders around the player's island while he is visiting.
Players can talk to him and open his shop.
He sells rare seeds for 30% cheaper than their normal price.

title: Pixel Burst
summary: My Roblox Studio plugin for building pixel bursts, UI explosions and reveal effects without remaking every particle by hand.
intro: Pixel Burst is a Studio plugin I made to speed up a type of effect I use often. The editor lets me build the effect visually and preview it before using it in a game.

cover.alt: Pixel Burst Studio plugin editor with effect layers and a live preview

made: Built as a Roblox Studio plugin.
details: Effects are built from separate layers with their own shape, timing and motion settings.
The live preview makes it easier to adjust the result without running the game every time.
Layers can be reordered, duplicated, hidden and edited separately.
The plugin generates code so the finished effect can be installed into a project.

title: Interface Work
summary: Two shop interfaces made in different styles: one cleaner and darker, and one based around studs and old-school Roblox UI.
intro: These are two shop interfaces I made in different styles. I do not use one fixed look for every game; I try to match the UI to the project.
made: Designed and built in Roblox Studio.
details: The cheese shop uses a cleaner dark style with a large item preview and clear purchase choices.
The seed shop uses studs, chunky borders, pixel text and bright rarity colours.
Both layouts keep the item, price and purchase state easy to read.
The interfaces were made for different games rather than being reskins of the same template.

media.alt: Cheese shop UI with item description and purchase buttons
media.title: Cheese shop

media.alt: Studded seed shop UI with seed rarity and purchase buttons
media.title: Studded seed shop

title: Combat Effects
summary: A group of combat effects I made while practising VFX, including fireballs, sword slashes and an explosion.
intro: These are a few combat effects I made in Roblox Studio. They are separate experiments rather than one full game system.

cover.alt: Fire and slash effect in Roblox Studio

details: The effects combine particles, trails, beams and timed movement.
Each clip was made as a separate practice piece.
I focused on impact, timing and keeping the effect readable during gameplay.
